scope: Expanded registered alternative-basis search over effectively specifiable reconstructions of the frozen bounded source and cross-feature classes. The search is broader than the original candidate universe but is not mathematically exhaustive over every possible language or ontology.

searched_bases.name: pure_relational_incidence
searched_bases.reason: Cannot recover commitment content, admissibility, or revision without reintroducing typed labels and transition constraints.

searched_bases.name: bare_transition_system
searched_bases.reason: Operational reachability is preserved but grounds, dependency, semantic version, and historical identity are lost.

searched_bases.name: provenance_only
searched_bases.reason: Ground and history records do not determine admissible evolution or current commitment content.

searched_bases.name: structural_causal_primitives
searched_bases.reason: Successful reconstruction requires typed endogenous commitments, intervention admissibility, dependency provenance, and versioned history matching RCCD commitments.

searched_bases.name: proof_theoretic_primitives
searched_bases.reason: Premises, discharge rules, proof-state transitions, supersession, and queryable derivation history reintroduce RCCD structure.

searched_bases.name: dependent_type_primitives
searched_bases.reason: Indexed commitments, admissible transports, dependency indices, and versioned historical witnesses reintroduce RCCD structure.

searched_bases.name: process_algebra_primitives
searched_bases.reason: Observable commitments, synchronization constraints, explicit schedulers, and trace provenance reintroduce RCCD structure.

searched_bases.name: coalgebraic_behavior_primitives
searched_bases.reason: Observation maps, constrained evolution, dependency observations, and historical recovery reintroduce RCCD structure.

searched_bases.name: information_flow_primitives
searched_bases.reason: Flows and channels alone do not distinguish accepted commitment revision from arbitrary state change.

searched_bases.name: algebraic_rewriting_primitives
searched_bases.reason: Typed terms, guarded rewrites, dependency certificates, and persistent rewrite history reintroduce RCCD structure.

searched_bases.name: categorical_process_primitives
searched_bases.reason: Objects and morphisms become adequate only after commitment interpretation, admissibility substructure, dependency decoration, and history indexing are supplied.

searched_bases.name: dynamical_system_primitives
searched_bases.reason: State and flow require commitment observables, admissibility constraints, explicit revision events, and history recovery.

searched_bases.name: event_sourced_database_primitives
searched_bases.reason: Schemas, invariants, dependency links, append-only revision semantics, and query contracts instantiate RCCD directly.

searched_bases.name: minimum_description_synthesized_basis
searched_bases.reason: Compression reduced surface syntax but moved equivalent commitments into decoder tables, canonicalizers, and certificates; total charged cost did not strictly improve.

automated_search.outcome: No successful strictly cheaper non-equivalent reconstruction was found under the registered preservation and charging rules.

surviving_countermodels.description: Two systems with identical reachable states and outputs but different grounds and dependency-sensitive revision permissions.
surviving_countermodels.effect: Rejects bare behavioral or transition equivalence.

surviving_countermodels.description: Two systems with the same current commitments but different supersession histories and future admissibility.
Two systems with extensionally equal current transitions but different semantic-version identities and later revision consequences.
Two hidden processes with equal observation traces but different commitment and intervention structure.
surviving_countermodels.effect: Rejects observational equivalence as full identity.

surviving_countermodels.description: Two concurrent systems differing only in a hidden scheduler that changes accepted revision order.

machinery_ledger: synthesis search programs and objective functions
nonclaims: every logically possible vocabulary has been searched
failure to find a replacement proves global impossibility
RCCD is already globally necessary or minimal
RCCD is unique or universally instantiated
